passport_meta.display_name: Миша Карпов — AI, продукты и рост ⛳

material_limitations: No community comments available in the corpus to assess audience resonance or pushback.
corpus_audit.author_vs_community_boundary: Clear. Only expert posts are present.

Background in Yandex, VK, Skyeng; hiring standards.

executive_summary.one_sentence_positioning: A serial entrepreneur and ex-CPO who provides highly practical, practitioner-level frameworks for transitioning from traditional product management to AI-assisted 'vibe coding' and autonomous agentic workflows.
executive_summary.strongest_system_value: Exceptional depth in practical AI-assisted development (Cursor, n8n, Lovable) and the organizational shift towards 'Builder PMs' and AI-agent orchestration.
executive_summary.weakest_system_value: Lacks deep technical ML/architecture insights, focusing entirely on the application layer, no-code/low-code tools, and management of AI agents.

worldview: AI agents are transitioning from tools to employees, requiring a shift in management mindset.
Product managers must evolve into 'Builder PMs' capable of executing code via AI.
Speed of experimentation (100 MVPs a year) beats perfect planning.
Backlogs should be experiment factories driven by AI, not graveyards of ideas.
expert_positioning.distinctive_angle: Combines deep C-level product management experience (ex-Yandex, VK, Skyeng) with hands-on, cutting-edge AI vibe coding and agent orchestration.
recurring_formats: Reflections on management and soft skills

what_the_expert_knows: How to build web and mobile apps from scratch using Cursor, Replit, and Lovable without traditional coding skills.
How to set up automated testing for vibe-coded applications.
How to use Claude Code for parallel agent workflows and verification loops.
how_they_reason: Prioritizes speed and iteration over perfect architecture ('Shame Driven Development').
Views AI as a co-founder/CTO rather than just an autocomplete tool.
missing_or_weak_subtopics: Security and governance of AI-generated code

what_the_expert_knows: How to build and manage a team of specialized AI agents (PM, scout, creator, designer).
How to use Conductor to turn a backlog into an autonomous experiment factory.
The management challenges of AI agents (e.g., agents 'slacking off' and needing AI managers).
how_they_reason: Treats AI agents as employees requiring management, clear roles, and orchestration, rather than simple scripts.
Believes the bottleneck is no longer hiring engineers, but rewiring human brains to manage digital teams.

what_the_expert_knows: How the PM role is evolving into a 'Builder PM' who executes code via AI.
How to set up an 'AI Chief of Staff' for personal and team productivity.
Frameworks for CPO skills, metrics hierarchy, and cross-departmental KPI alignment.
how_they_reason: Combines traditional C-level product strategy (metrics, OKRs) with aggressive AI automation.
Advocates for the 'loop' where AI helps build the next version of the AI product.
missing_or_weak_subtopics: Deep financial modeling for AI inference

value_dimensions.rationale: Consistently shares exact tools (Cursor, n8n, Lovable, Conductor), workflows, and even downloadable n8n templates for immediate use.
High density of actionable frameworks, especially regarding the shift to 'Builder PMs' and managing AI agents as employees.
Provides strong heuristics for deciding when to use AI agents, how to structure metrics, and how to transition PM roles.
Runs an 'Anti-hype AI' digest that filters out noise, focusing on what actually works in production (e.g., noting that 95% of autonomous agents failed without an AI manager).

matrix_cells.notes: Excellent source for non-technical PMs and founders learning to build apps using Cursor, Replit, and Lovable.
Signature insights on treating AI agents as employees, including the necessity of 'AI managers' to prevent agent degradation, and using Conductor for backlog execution.
Strong practical advice on the evolution of the PM role to include code execution via AI, and setting up personal AI Chiefs of Staff.
Solid, battle-tested frameworks for CPO skills, metrics hierarchy, and cross-departmental alignment from his time at Skyeng/VK.

query_intent_fit.why: Provides step-by-step guidance, tool stacks (Cursor, Lovable, Replit), and mindset shifts for non-technical people to start building apps.
limitations: Focuses on rapid prototyping and MVP building rather than enterprise-grade scalable architecture.

query_intent_fit.why: Shares cutting-edge workflows using Claude Code, Conductor, and multi-agent orchestration to automate backlog execution.
Deeply explores the integration of AI agents as 'employees' (AI Chief of Staff, AI PMs, AI researchers) into daily human workflows.
Offers strong CPO-level frameworks for metrics and strategy, combined with rapid AI prototyping.
limitations: More focused on the execution/building phase than deep user research for AI specifically.

content_quality_distribution.notes: Detailed breakdowns of agent orchestration, Cursor workflows, and CPO management frameworks.
Frequent sharing of tool stacks, n8n templates, and step-by-step guides for vibe coding.
Promotes his AI Product Club, ProductCamp, and shares 'Anti-hype AI' news digests.
Reflections on leadership, soft skills, and startup culture.

limitations: Focuses on rapid MVP creation rather than enterprise architecture.

signature_insights.insight: The product backlog should transition from a 'graveyard of ideas' to an 'experiment factory' where AI agents (via tools like Conductor) autonomously test, build, and iterate on ideas.
signature_insights.why_it_matters_for_experts_panel: Provides a paradigm shift in how PMs and founders should view task management in the age of autonomous AI agents.

signature_insights.insight: AI agents degrade without management. When deploying a swarm of agents to build software, 'slacking off' occurs, necessitating the creation of 'AI Manager' roles to coordinate and push the worker agents.
signature_insights.why_it_matters_for_experts_panel: Highlights a critical, non-obvious failure mode in multi-agent orchestration and offers a structural solution.

signature_insights.insight: The role of the Product Manager is evolving into the 'Builder PM', who uses AI tools (Cursor, Claude) to execute code, submit PRs, and bypass the traditional engineering bottleneck.
signature_insights.why_it_matters_for_experts_panel: Signals a major shift in PM skill requirements and provides a practical pipeline for non-technical PMs to start shipping code.

signature_insights.insight: Shame Driven Development (SDD): Shipping extremely raw, embarrassing MVPs forces the team to rapidly fix and polish the product due to the immediate discomfort of public exposure.
signature_insights.why_it_matters_for_experts_panel: A highly practical, psychological hack for overcoming perfectionism and accelerating time-to-market.

practical_patterns.pattern: AI Chief of Staff via n8n and Telegram
practical_patterns.when_to_use: When needing to automate personal knowledge management, calendar tracking, and focus alignment.
failure_modes: Over-engineering the automation instead of keeping it simple.

practical_patterns.when_to_use: When a non-technical PM needs to build and ship a feature directly.
failure_modes: Failing to use a verification loop or peer-review between different LLMs before committing code.

practical_patterns.when_to_use: When cross-departmental KPIs conflict and A/B testing is noisy due to low traffic.
failure_modes: Tracking only final metrics instead of leading/predictive metrics.

claims_and_positions.claim: The bottleneck in tech is no longer hiring engineers, but rewiring human brains to manage digital AI teams.
claims_and_positions.decision_relevance: Shifts organizational focus from recruitment to AI orchestration and management training.

claims_and_positions.claim: If a product does not have a 'loop' where AI helps build the next version of the product, it is already obsolete.
claims_and_positions.decision_relevance: Encourages integrating AI not just as a feature, but into the core development cycle of the product itself.

best_use_cases: Learning how to transition from a traditional PM to a 'Builder PM' using AI tools.
Designing workflows for multi-agent orchestration and AI teams.
Setting up personal productivity automations using n8n and Claude.
Understanding CPO-level metrics and cross-functional KPI alignment.
bad_use_cases: Deep technical architecture for scalable ML models.
Enterprise security and governance for AI deployments.
source_selection_hints: Select this expert when the user is a PM, founder, or non-technical leader looking to build apps or automate workflows using AI agents.
expected_overlap_hypotheses: High overlap with other 'vibe coding' and AI productivity influencers.
Moderate overlap with traditional product management experts on topics like OKRs and metrics.

admission_implications.verdict_rationale: Misha Karpov provides exceptionally high-quality, practitioner-level insights into the rapidly evolving field of AI-assisted development ('vibe coding') and agent orchestration. His transition from a traditional CPO to an AI builder offers a highly relevant perspective for PMs and founders.
unique_value_claims_to_verify_against_ma: Frameworks for managing AI agents as employees (e.g., dealing with agent degradation).
The 'Builder PM' pipeline using Cursor and Claude.
likely_duplicate_areas_to_check: General product management advice (OKRs, metrics, hiring).
minimum_followup_probe_queries: How does the expert handle security and scalability when deploying vibe-coded applications to production?
risk_notes: He frequently promotes his own paid courses and events (AI Product Club, ProductCamp), which introduces some commercial bias, though he consistently provides free, actionable value alongside the promotions.

abstentions: Community signal could not be evaluated as no comments were included in the corpus.
